feedback.text: As a long time customer and supporter of the gaming industry, I am writing to express my deep concern about the ongoing practice of publishers and developers removing access to games that consumers have legally purchased. I strongly support the goals of the Stop Killing Games movement, and I urge you to include clear, enforceable protections in the Digital Fairness Act to prevent this harmful and increasingly common behavior. Digital games are not just products they are cultural works, artistic experiences, and often community hubs. When a game is removed, disabled, or made unplayable despite being bought legally by customers it undermines trust and damages the integrity of digital ownership. Many consumers, including myself, invest not just money but also time, memories, and emotional value into these experiences. Shutting down a game with no alternative means of access or preservation erases part of that cultural and personal history. Its especially concerning when no refund or offline mode is offered, and when there is no legal pathway for community preservation. I respectfully ask that the Digital Fairness Act include provisions such as: Legal protections to prevent the deliberate removal or disabling of purchased games. Requirements for publishers to offer an offline version or refund if online services are discontinued. Support for preservation by libraries, museums, and dedicated nonprofits, even if it means bypassing DRM in a responsible, non commercial way. Digital consumers deserve the same basic rights and stability that physical media once guaranteed. The Digital Fairness Act is a crucial opportunity to ensure that digital ownership actually means ownership. Please stand with consumers and help stop the unnecessary destruction of games. Let us preserve our digital heritage, and ensure fairness for all. Sincerely, A concerned consumer and gaming enthusiast
